body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Primitive

The scenario exercises `streaming_output` — the agent emits assistant content across multiple partial flushes before a final turn-end signal, and the irrlicht invariant is that those partial flushes must NOT trigger a premature `→ ready` (no `ready → working → ready → working` flicker). The canonical signal is `ready → working → ready` with state pinned at `working` across every flush. The matching `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` key is `streaming_output` (= true).

## agent_supports: yes

Aider supports streaming output. `capabilities.json` records `streaming_output: true`, and aider's docs confirm `--stream` (streaming pretty LLM responses) is enabled by default and only auto-disabled for models that lack support. In aider's transcript shape the relevant 'partial flush' unit is per-model-call: under `--yes-always`, a single `####` user turn drives multiple model calls (e.g. an initial reply, then auto-accepted file-adds, then a follow-up analysis), each delimited by a `> Tokens: …` line. The committed regression transcript `replaydata/agents/aider/regression/yes-always-multi-step/transcript.md` shows exactly this: two prose blocks (lines 12–20 and 30–34), each terminated by its own `> Tokens:` line within one `####` turn.

## daemon_capability: full

The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD` + `RawLineParser` over `.aider.chat.history.md` (`agent.go:45-51`). The parser buffers plain prose lines silently (`parser.go:137-143`, returns nil) and emits `assistant_message` — NOT `turn_done` — at each `> Tokens:` line (`closeModelCall`, `parser.go:153-188`), keeping `turnOpen` true across model calls (verified by `TestParser_MultiModelCall_KeepsTurnOpen`). `IsAgentDone()` (`session.go:461-496`) treats only `turn_done` (and the Claude-specific `assistant`/`assistant_output` fallback) as done — aider's `assistant_message` is NOT matched, so per-call flushes cannot flip the session to `ready`. The session holds `working` across every back-to-back flush, which is exactly the no-flicker invariant the scenario pins. For the common case the daemon emits the lifecycle events the spec requires (`ready → working`, no intermediate `→ ready`, then a single `working → ready` at turn end), so the daemon axis is `full`.

The spec invariant — "partial flushes must NOT flip the session to ready mid-turn" — is met by the parser as it exists today for completed per-model-call prose blocks. aider has no token-level streaming trace at all (terminal streaming is TUI-only; the markdown history is written as completed message blocks — confirmed against aider's `--stream` / `--chat-history-file` docs), so there is no spec-required streaming-chunk event for the daemon to mis-handle. There is no recorded `events.jsonl` line showing the daemon mis-parsing a real trace, so this is NOT a `bug`; the residual timing hazard is captured as a caveat (below) rather than a downgrade.

## driver_capability: ready

The recipe needs only `send`, `wait_turn` (×2, to span a genuine multi-model-call turn driven by the `--auto-test --test-cmd false` reflection loop), and `sleep` — all implemented by `scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`. No gapped primitive (restart / reset_session / resume / start_session / exit_clean / sigkill / keys / session / fork) is required, so the driver is `ready`.

## Why not daemon_capability: bug

The one carve-out is timing, not mis-handling of a trace: aider's only clean end-of-turn signal is `IdleFlush`, which synthesizes `turn_done` after the history is quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` = 1500ms (`parser.go:54`, `216-229`). A slow remote model call that leaves the file quiet >1500ms BETWEEN flushes can make IdleFlush synthesize a premature `turn_done` → premature `→ ready` → flicker. But this is a timing-window artifact of the IdleFlush heuristic for non-spec-representable token streaming, not the daemon mis-parsing a trace it should handle; no cited `events.jsonl` line demonstrates a real mis-handling. Per the authoring rule (caveats over downgrades; reserve `bug` for a spec-required observable the daemon mis-handles), this stays `daemon_capability: full` with the hazard recorded as a caveat. The recipe targets the no-flicker IDEAL and the `streaming_window` invariant in a recording is what would surface any such premature-ready drift.

## Matrix

The coverage row already carries the two-axis schema (`agent_supports: yes`, `daemon_capability: unknown`, `driver_capability: ready`). This assessment resolves the `unknown` daemon axis to `full`; the maintainer should update the coverage row's `daemon_capability` from `unknown` → `full`.
caveats: metric-drift-downstream: aider's end-of-turn is synthesized by IdleFlush after 1500ms of transcript quiet (parser.go:54, 216-229); a slow remote model call that goes quiet >1500ms BETWEEN flushes can fire a premature turn_done → premature ready, flickering the no-flicker invariant the scenario pins. Holds for normal back-to-back flushes (the common case) but is not airtight against a slow remote call.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: the scenario's canonical text describes Claude/JSONL assistant_streaming chunks (parser.go:288, non-terminal stop_reason); aider has no equivalent JSONL streaming event — its 'partial flush' unit is the per-model-call prose block terminated by a `> Tokens:` line, so the recipe must drive multi-model-call prose within one turn (via the --auto-test reflection loop), not token-level streaming.

sources.note: streaming_output: true — confirms the primitive-exercised capability key

closeModelCall emits assistant_message (not turn_done) per `> Tokens:` line; turnOpen stays true; IdleFlush (aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter=1500ms) is the only clean end-of-turn signal (lines 54, 153-188, 216-229)

IsAgentDone (461-496): only turn_done (or Claude's assistant/assistant_output fallback) is 'done'; aider's assistant_message is NOT matched, so per-call flushes keep the session working — the no-flicker invariant holds for back-to-back flushes (daemon_capability: full)
TestParser_MultiModelCall_KeepsTurnOpen + TestParser_IdleFlush_AfterMultipleModelCalls_EmitsTurnDone: turn stays open across multiple `> Tokens:` lines, closes only on idle flush
real transcript: two prose blocks within one #### turn, each terminated by its own `> Tokens:` line — the aider analog of partial flushes
--stream enabled by default (TUI streaming); --chat-history-file defaults to .aider.chat.history.md — file is written as completed message blocks, not token-by-token
aider streaming behaviour and chat-history markdown logging
